item.name: admin
auth

Get current admin profile. Requires JWT authentication.
Change admin password. Requires JWT authentication.

item.name: giveaways

formdata.value: Win the latest iPhone 15 Pro Max - 256GB Space Black

formdata.description: ACTIVE (publish now - default), DRAFT (save as draft), or COMPLETED. Only one ACTIVE giveaway allowed - returns 409 error if one exists.

request.description: Create a new giveaway with ticket packages. Upload bannerImage as file. Default status is ACTIVE (publish immediately). Use status='DRAFT' to save without publishing. If trying to create ACTIVE when one already exists, returns 409 Conflict error. System auto-generates title, basePrice, savePercentage, and badgeText for each package.

query.description: Filter by status: DRAFT, ACTIVE, or COMPLETED (optional)

request.description:
Get paginated list of giveaways. Use status query parameter to filter (DRAFT, ACTIVE, COMPLETED).

When status=ACTIVE:
• Returns detailed stats for each giveaway:
  - totalRevenue: Sum of COMPLETED orders
  - totalSales: Count of COMPLETED orders
  - salesProgress: Percentage (0-100%)
  - totalEmails: Count of unique customer emails
  - dailyAverageSales: totalSales / days_since_created
  - conversionRate: (totalSales / totalTickets) * 100
• Returns recent 3 purchasers:
  - fullName, email, instagram
  - ticketRange (coupon codes)
  - purchaseDate

When status=COMPLETED:
• Returns winner details:
  - winner object: couponCode, fullName, email, instagram
  - drawDate, totalTickets, ticketsSold

For status=DRAFT:
• Returns minimal data: id, title, bannerImage, status, createdAt

Get overview statistics for the giveaways dashboard.

========================================
📊 RETURNS
========================================

• **totalRevenue:** Sum of revenue from all ACTIVE giveaways (COMPLETED orders only)
• **totalTicketsSold:** Sum of tickets sold across all ACTIVE giveaways
• **totalTicketsAvailable:** Sum of total available tickets across all ACTIVE giveaways
• **activeGiveawaysCount:** Number of ACTIVE giveaways
• **upcomingDraws:** Array of ACTIVE giveaways sorted by days remaining (closest first)
  - id, title, drawDate, daysRemaining
  - ticketsSold, totalTickets

========================================
✅ EXAMPLE RESPONSE
========================================

{
  "message": "Giveaways overview stats retrieved successfully",
  "data": {
    "totalRevenue": 45200.50,
    "totalTicketsSold": 850,
    "totalTicketsAvailable": 1000,
    "activeGiveawaysCount": 1,
    "upcomingDraws": [
      {
        "id": "uuid-1",
        "title": "Bathroom Renovation Giveaway",
        "drawDate": "2026-05-10T00:00:00Z",
        "daysRemaining": 12,
        "ticketsSold": 850,
        "totalTickets": 1000
      }
    ]
  }
}

Get a single giveaway by ID with all details.

Returns:
• All giveaway info
• Ticket packages
• Stats (if ACTIVE):
  - totalRevenue: Sum of COMPLETED orders
  - totalSales: Count of COMPLETED orders
  - salesProgress: Percentage (0-100%)
  - totalEmails: Count of unique customer emails
  - dailyAverageSales: totalSales / days_since_created
  - conversionRate: (totalSales / totalTickets) * 100
• Recent 3 purchasers (if ACTIVE):
  - fullName, email, instagram
  - ticketRange (coupon codes)
  - purchaseDate

formdata.value: Updated description - Win the latest iPhone 15 Pro Max

formdata.description: Update status to ACTIVE to publish the giveaway. Returns 409 error if another active giveaway exists.

request.description: Update giveaway details. All fields are optional. Can update title, description, totalTickets, drawDate, drawTime, bannerImage (file upload), status, and packages. If packages are provided, all existing packages will be replaced with the new ones. Set status='ACTIVE' to publish a draft - returns 409 error if another active giveaway exists.

🔴 WRITE OPERATION - Automatically draw a random winner and save to database.

This is a POST request because it modifies the database:
• Randomly selects a winner from VALID coupons
• Sets giveaway status to COMPLETED
• Persists winnerCouponId to database

========================================
🎰 PROCESS
========================================

1. Retrieves all VALID coupons for the giveaway
2. Randomly selects one winner (cryptographically secure)
3. Updates giveaway in database:
   - Sets winnerCouponId
   - Sets status to COMPLETED
4. Returns giveaway with winner details

========================================
✅ SUCCESS RESPONSE (200)
========================================

{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Winner drawn successfully",
  "data": {
    "giveaway": {
      "id": "uuid-1",
      "title": "€10,000 Cash Giveaway",
      "status": "COMPLETED",
      "winnerCouponId": "coupon-id",
      "winnerCoupon": {...},
      ...
    },
    "winner": {
      "couponCode": "GW-A1B2C3-042",
      "fullName": "John Doe",
      "email": "john@example.com",
      "instagram": "@john_builds"
    }
  }
}

========================================
❌ ERROR RESPONSES
========================================

❌ 404 - Giveaway not found
❌ 400 - No valid coupons available
  (All coupons have been marked as VOID)
❌ 500 - Server error

========================================
💡 USAGE
========================================

1. Admin clicks "Draw Winner" button
2. System randomly selects from valid coupons
3. Winner saved in database (winnerCouponId)
4. Giveaway marked as COMPLETED
5. Winner details returned to frontend

Manually select winner by providing a valid coupon code.

========================================
📋 REQUEST BODY
========================================

{
  "couponCode": "GW-A1B2C3-042"
}

========================================
🔍 VALIDATION
========================================

1. **Coupon must exist:** Returns 404 if coupon code not found
2. **Must belong to giveaway:** Returns 400 if coupon belongs to different giveaway
3. **Must be VALID:** Returns 400 if coupon status is VOID
4. **No duplicate winners:** Only one winner can be selected per giveaway

========================================
✅ SUCCESS RESPONSE (200)
========================================

{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Winner selected successfully",
  "data": {
    "giveaway": {
      "id": "uuid-1",
      "title": "€10,000 Cash Giveaway",
      "status": "COMPLETED",
      "winnerCouponId": "coupon-id",
      "winnerCoupon": {...},
      ...
    },
    "winner": {
      "couponCode": "GW-A1B2C3-042",
      "fullName": "John Doe",
      "email": "john@example.com",
      "instagram": "@john_builds"
    }
  }
}

========================================
❌ ERROR RESPONSES
========================================

❌ 400 - Missing couponCode in body
❌ 404 - Coupon not found
❌ 400 - Coupon does not belong to this giveaway
❌ 400 - Coupon is not valid (VOID status)
❌ 500 - Server error

========================================
💡 USAGE
========================================

1. Admin searches for participant coupon code
2. Enters coupon code in request body
3. System validates and selects winner
4. Giveaway status changes to COMPLETED
5. Winner announced

========================================
📝 HOW TO FIND COUPON CODES
========================================

1. Go to Entry Details Preview
2. Find participant ticket number (e.g., #482-485)
3. Extract coupon code from ticket range
4. Send in this endpoint as couponCode parameter

📧 Send winner notification email to the selected winner.

========================================
📋 OVERVIEW
========================================

Sends a professional email notification to the giveaway winner informing them that they have won. This endpoint should be called AFTER a winner has been drawn/selected.

========================================
✅ SUCCESS RESPONSE (200)
========================================

{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Winner notification email sent successfully",
  "data": {
    "success": true,
    "message": "Winner notification email sent successfully",
    "emailSent": true,
    "winner": {
      "fullName": "John Doe",
      "email": "john@example.com",
      "couponCode": "GW-A1B2C3-042"
    },
    "emailResult": {
      "success": true,
      "messageId": "<message-id@smtp.gmail.com>"
    }
  }
}

========================================
📧 EMAIL CONTENT
========================================

The winner receives a beautiful HTML email with:
• Congratulations message
• Giveaway title
• Winning coupon code (highlighted)
• Professional design with your brand colors
• Instructions for claiming the prize

========================================
❌ ERROR RESPONSES
========================================

❌ 404 - Giveaway not found
❌ 400 - No winner has been selected for this giveaway yet
  (Must draw/select winner first)
❌ 500 - Email sending failed (check SMTP configuration)
❌ 500 - Server error

========================================
💡 USAGE FLOW
========================================

1. Admin draws winner (draw-winner or select-winner)
2. Admin clicks "Inquire" or "Notify Winner" button
3. System sends email to winner's registered email
4. Winner receives congratulations email
5. Winner knows they won and can claim prize

========================================
⚙️ SMTP CONFIGURATION REQUIRED
========================================

Make sure the following environment variables are set:

• SMTP_HOST (e.g., smtp.gmail.com)
• SMTP_PORT (e.g., 587)
• SMTP_USER (your email address)
• SMTP_PASS (app password for Gmail)
• EMAIL_FROM_NAME (e.g., Toufim)

For Gmail, create an App Password:
https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/185833

========================================
📝 NOTES
========================================

• Can be called multiple times (re-sends email)
• Email is sent immediately
• Winner must have valid email address
• Check spam folder if email not received
Delete a giveaway and all its ticket packages.

item.description: Giveaway management endpoints - create, read, update, delete giveaways and manage ticket packages

item.name: vouchers

request.description:
Create a new voucher using form-data.

FORM-DATA FIELDS:
• code (text) - Unique voucher code (e.g., 'AMBER-FALL-27')
• discountType (text) - 'PERCENTAGE' or 'FLAT'
• discountValue (text) - Number: 1-100 for PERCENTAGE, currency amount for FLAT
• usageLimit (text) - Maximum number of times voucher can be used
• expirationDate (text) - ISO date format (e.g., '2026-12-31')
• status (text) - 'DRAFT' or 'ACTIVE' (default: ACTIVE)

RESPONSE:
✅ 201 - Voucher created successfully
❌ 409 - Voucher with this code already exists (user-friendly error message)
❌ 400 - Validation error (e.g., PERCENTAGE > 100)

USAGE TRACKING:
usedCount starts at 0 and increments with each order, auto-expires when usedCount >= usageLimit.

query.description: Filter by status: DRAFT, ACTIVE, or EXPIRED (optional)

request.description: Get paginated list of vouchers. Returns code, discount details, usage stats, and status. Use status query parameter to filter (DRAFT, ACTIVE, EXPIRED). Remove status to get all.

Get voucher overview/statistics for the admin dashboard.

========================================
📊 RETURNS
========================================

• **totalDiscountsApplied:** Sum of all discount amounts from orders that used vouchers (calculated from: package.price - order.totalAmount)
• **activeVouchers:** Count of vouchers with ACTIVE status
• **totalVouchers:** Count of all vouchers (DRAFT, ACTIVE, EXPIRED)
• **ordersUsingVouchers:** Count of orders that applied vouchers
• **averageDiscountPerOrder:** Average discount amount per order
• **topActiveVouchers:** Last 5 recent active vouchers with usage details (id, code, discountType, discountValue, usedCount, usageLimit, expirationDate, order count)

========================================
✅ EXAMPLE RESPONSE
========================================

{
  "message": "Voucher overview retrieved successfully",
  "data": {
    "totalDiscountsApplied": 12450.00,
    "activeVouchers": 18,
    "totalVouchers": 24,
    "ordersUsingVouchers": 156,
    "averageDiscountPerOrder": 79.81,
    "topActiveVouchers": [
      {
        "id": "uuid-1",
        "code": "SAVE20",
        "discountType": "PERCENTAGE",
        "discountValue": "20",
        "usedCount": 45,
        "usageLimit": 100,
        "expirationDate": "2026-12-31T00:00:00Z",
        "_count": {
          "orders": 45
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}

========================================
💡 USE CASE
========================================
Use this endpoint to populate dashboard cards:
- "Total Discounts Applied" card: Use totalDiscountsApplied
- "Active Vouchers" card: Use activeVouchers
- Display top performing vouchers in a table
Get a single voucher by ID with detailed usage information including all orders that used this voucher.

Update voucher details using form-data. All fields are optional.

FORM-DATA FIELDS (সব ঐচ্ছিক):
• code (text) - Update voucher code
• discountType (text) - 'PERCENTAGE' or 'FLAT'
• discountValue (text) - Number: 1-100 for PERCENTAGE, currency amount for FLAT
• usageLimit (text) - Update maximum usage limit
• expirationDate (text) - Update expiration date (ISO format: '2027-01-31')
• status (text) - 'DRAFT', 'ACTIVE', or 'EXPIRED'

URL PARAMETERS:
:voucherId - UUID of the voucher to update

RESPONSE:
✅ 200 - Voucher updated successfully
❌ 409 - Voucher with this code already exists (when changing code)
❌ 404 - Voucher not found

নোট: শুধুমাত্র যে ফিল্ড পরিবর্তন করতে চান সেটি পাঠান। বাকি ফিল্ড আগের মতোই থাকবে।
Delete a voucher. Only allowed if the voucher has not been used in any orders. If voucher has been used, mark it as EXPIRED instead.

item.description: Voucher management endpoints - create, read, update, delete discount vouchers with draft support

item.name: services

formdata.value: Detailed structural breakdown and workflow expectations...

formdata.description: Base price in EUR (required, e.g. 0.00 for free estimates)
Category in Dutch - see docs for valid values (required)
ACTIVE, COMPLETED, or DRAFT (optional, default: ACTIVE)

request.description:
Create a new service using form-data.

FORM-DATA FIELDS:
• name (text) - Service name
• description (text) - Detailed description
• basePrice (text) - Base price in EUR (e.g., '0.00')
• category (text) - Category in Dutch. Valid values:
  - Loodgietersdiensten
  - Dakdekkersdiensten
  - Stukadoorsdienst
  - Elektrische diensten
  - Tegelservices
  - Complete vloeroplossingen op één plek
  - Schoonmaakdiensten
  - Algemene service
• status (text) - 'ACTIVE', 'COMPLETED', or 'DRAFT' (default: ACTIVE)
• bannerImage (file) - Banner image (optional, max 5MB)
• galleryImages (files) - Gallery images (optional, max 10, 5MB each)

RESPONSE:
✅ 201 - Service created successfully
❌ 400 - Validation error

NOTE: Slug is auto-generated from service name

query.description: Filter by status: ACTIVE, COMPLETED, or DRAFT (optional)

request.description:
Get paginated list of services.

QUERY PARAMETERS:
• page (number) - Page number (default: 1)
• limit (number) - Items per page (default: 10)
• category (string) - Filter by category (optional, Dutch name)
• status (string) - Filter by status: ACTIVE, COMPLETED, or DRAFT (optional)

RETURNS:
• services: Array of service objects (category in Dutch)
• pagination: { page, limit, total, totalPages }

Each service includes: id, name, slug, description, basePrice, category (Dutch), bannerImage, galleryImages[], status, createdAt, updatedAt

Get a single service by ID with complete details.

URL PARAMETERS:
:serviceId - Service UUID

RETURNS:
• Complete service object with all fields
• Category in Dutch (e.g., 'Dakdekkersdiensten')
• Banner image URL
• Gallery images array

RESPONSE:
✅ 200 - Service details
❌ 404 - Service not found

formdata.description: Additional gallery images - appends to existing (optional)

request.description:
Update service details using form-data. All fields are optional.

FORM-DATA FIELDS (all optional):
• name (text) - Update service name (slug will auto-regenerate)
• description (text) - Update description
• basePrice (text) - Update base price
• category (text) - Update category (Dutch name)
• status (text) - 'ACTIVE', 'COMPLETED', or 'DRAFT'
• bannerImage (file) - Replace banner image
• galleryImages (files) - Add more gallery images (appends to existing)

URL PARAMETERS:
:serviceId - Service UUID

RESPONSE:
✅ 200 - Service updated successfully
❌ 404 - Service not found
❌ 400 - Validation error

NOTE: Only send fields you want to change. Gallery images append, not replace.

Delete a service permanently.

URL PARAMETERS:
:serviceId - Service UUID

RESPONSE:
✅ 200 - Service deleted successfully
❌ 404 - Service not found

WARNING: This action is irreversible. Consider setting status to DRAFT instead of deleting.

Remove specific images from service gallery.

REQUEST BODY (Raw JSON):
{
  "imageUrls": ["url1", "url2"]
}

FIELDS:
• imageUrls (array) - Array of image URLs to remove (must match exactly)

URL PARAMETERS:
:serviceId - UUID of the service

RESPONSE:
✅ 200 - Gallery images removed successfully
❌ 404 - Service not found
❌ 400 - Invalid imageUrls format

নোট: Banner image মুছতে এই endpoint ব্যবহার করবেন না। Update endpoint দিয়ে নতুন banner আপলোড করুন।

item.description: Service management endpoints - create, read, update, delete services with banner and gallery images

item.name: leads

query.description: Filter by status: PENDING, CONTACTED, COMPLETED, QUOTED, CLOSED (optional)

request.description:
Get all leads with optional status filter (Admin only).

========================================
🔍 QUERY PARAMETERS (All Optional)
========================================

status - Filter leads by status. Valid values:
  • PENDING - New leads not yet contacted
  • CONTACTED - Leads that have been contacted
  • QUOTED - Leads with quote sent
  • COMPLETED - Completed work
  • CLOSED - Closed leads (won't proceed)

Example:
  GET /api/admin/leads?status=PENDING
  GET /api/admin/leads?status=CONTACTED
  GET /api/admin/leads  (no filter, returns all)

========================================
✅ SUCCESS RESPONSE (200)
========================================

{
  "success": true,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "fullName": "Jan de Vries",
      "email": "jan@example.nl",
      "phone": "+31612345678",
      "serviceType": "Dakdekkersdiensten",  // Dutch name
      "address": "Kouwstraat 121, Amsterdam",
      "projectDetails": "Project description...",
      "referenceImages": [
        "http://localhost:3000/uploads/lead-123.jpg"
      ],
      "status": "PENDING",
      "createdAt": "2026-04-28T10:30:00.000Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-04-28T10:30:00.000Z"
    }
  ]
}

========================================
📋 RESPONSE DETAILS
========================================

• Ordered by creation date (newest first)
• serviceType returned in Dutch (e.g., 'Dakdekkersdiensten')
• referenceImages are absolute URLs
• Empty array [] if no leads found

========================================
📊 STATUS VALUES REFERENCE
========================================

PENDING → New lead, awaiting contact
CONTACTED → First contact made
QUOTED → Quote/estimate sent
COMPLETED → Work finished successfully
CLOSED → Lead closed (declined/not interested)

========================================
💡 FRONTEND USAGE
========================================

• Use for lead dashboard/list view
• Filter by status to show different tabs
• Display newest leads first
• Show status with color coding

Get a single lead by ID with complete details.

URL PARAMETERS:
:id - Lead UUID

RETURNS:
• Complete lead object with all fields
• ServiceType in Dutch
• Reference images array

RESPONSE:
✅ 200 - Lead details
❌ 404 - Lead not found

Update lead status (change lead workflow state).

⚠️ IMPORTANT: Admin inputs status in Dutch. Backend also accepts English for backward compatibility.

========================================
📋 VALID STATUS VALUES (Dutch / English)
========================================

1. In afwachting / PENDING
   └─ New lead, not yet contacted
   └─ Default status when lead is submitted
   └─ Use this when lead needs follow-up

2. Gecontacteerd / CONTACTED
   └─ First contact made with customer
   └─ Initial call/email sent
   └─ Customer is aware of the lead

3. Geoffreerd / QUOTED
   └─ Quote/estimate sent to customer
   └─ Pricing provided
   └─ Waiting for customer decision

4. Voltooid / COMPLETED
   └─ Work completed successfully
   └─ Customer satisfied
   └─ Project finished

5. Gesloten / CLOSED
   └─ Lead closed without work
   └─ Customer declined or not interested
   └─ Won't proceed with this lead

========================================
📊 TYPICAL STATUS FLOW
========================================

In afwachting → Gecontacteerd → Geoffreerd → Voltooid
                                             ↓
                                          Gesloten

========================================
📝 REQUEST BODY (Raw JSON)
========================================

Recommended - Dutch (admin sends):
{
  "status": "Gecontacteerd"
}

Also accepted - English (backward compatibility):
{
  "status": "CONTACTED"
}

Both work! Backend converts to English → stores → returns Dutch

========================================
🔗 URL PARAMETERS
========================================

:id - Lead UUID (required)

========================================
✅ SUCCESS RESPONSE (200)
========================================

{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Lead status updated successfully",
  "data": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "status": "Gecontacteerd",  // ← Always returned in Dutch
    "fullName": "Jan de Vries",
    "email": "jan@example.nl",
    "phone": "+31612345678",
    "serviceType": "Dakdekkersdiensten",  // ← Also in Dutch
    "address": "Kouwstraat 121, Amsterdam",
    "projectDetails": "...",
    "referenceImages": ["http://..."],
    "createdAt": "2026-04-28T...",
    "updatedAt": "2026-04-28T..."
  }
}

========================================
❌ ERROR RESPONSES
========================================

400 - Invalid status value:
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Ongeldige status: INVALID_STATUS"
}

400 - Missing status:
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Status is required"
}

404 - Lead not found:
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Lead not found"
}

========================================
💡 FRONTEND USAGE TIPS
========================================

• Use dropdown/select with Dutch status values (recommended)
• Backend accepts both English and Dutch
• Response always returns Dutch for frontend display
• Show status flow visualization to users
• Display status with color coding:
  - In afwachting (PENDING): Yellow/Orange
  - Gecontacteerd (CONTACTED): Blue
  - Geoffreerd (QUOTED): Purple
  - Voltooid (COMPLETED): Green
  - Gesloten (CLOSED): Red/Gray

formdata.description: CSV or XLSX file with leads data (required, max 25MB) | Column Order: fullName,email,phone,serviceType,address,projectDetails,dateReceived,status

request.description:
Bulk upload leads from CSV or Excel file (Admin only).

========================================
📋 FILE FORMAT (CSV or XLSX)
========================================

Supported formats:
• CSV (.csv) - Comma-separated values
• XLSX (.xlsx) - Microsoft Excel format (recommended)

⚠️ IMPORTANT: Column Order Must Be Exact!

The file MUST have these columns in this EXACT ORDER:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ COLUMN ORDER (Left to Right in Excel/CSV)              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 1. fullName        (Column A - Required)               │
│ 2. email           (Column B - Required)               │
│ 3. phone           (Column C - Required)               │
│ 4. serviceType     (Column D - Required)               │
│ 5. address         (Column E - Required)               │
│ 6. projectDetails  (Column F - Optional)               │
│ 7. dateReceived    (Column G - Optional)               │
│ 8. status          (Column H - Optional)               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Field Details:
• fullName - Customer full name (required)
• email - Customer email address (required)
• phone - Customer phone number (required)
• serviceType - Service type in Dutch (required)
• address - Project address (required)
• projectDetails - Project description (optional)
• dateReceived - Date when lead was received (optional, format: YYYY-MM-DD)
• status - Lead status (optional, default: PENDING)

📋 QUICK REFERENCE - Copy this header row:
fullName,email,phone,serviceType,address,projectDetails,dateReceived,status

⚠️ IMPORTANT: Optional Fields
• dateReceived: Use this when uploading leads from other sources (phone, WhatsApp, offline)
  - Format: YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 2026-04-20)
  - If not provided or empty, current date will be used
  - Invalid dates will cause row to fail with error

• status: Set initial status for each lead
  - Valid values (English): PENDING, CONTACTED, QUOTED, COMPLETED, CLOSED
  - Valid values (Dutch): In afwachting, Gecontacteerd, Geoffreerd, Voltooid, Gesloten
  - Accepts both English and Dutch (Dutch recommended for consistency)
  - Case-insensitive for English (pending, PENDING, Pending all work)
  - If not provided or empty, defaults to PENDING
  - Invalid status will cause row to fail with error

========================================
✅ VALID SERVICE TYPES (Dutch)
========================================

1. Loodgietersdiensten
2. Dakdekkersdiensten
3. Stukadoorsdienst
4. Elektrische diensten
5. Tegelservices
6. Complete vloeroplossingen op één plek
7. Schoonmaakdiensten
8. Algemene service

========================================
📄 CSV/XLSX FILE EXAMPLE
========================================

Row 1 (Header - MUST match exactly):
fullName,email,phone,serviceType,address,projectDetails,dateReceived,status

Row 2+ (Data examples):
Jan de Vries,jan@example.nl,+31612345678,Dakdekkersdiensten,"Kouwstraat 121, Amsterdam","Need roof repair",2026-04-20,In afwachting
Maria Jansen,maria@example.nl,+31687654321,Loodgietersdiensten,"Herengracht 456, Amsterdam","Bathroom plumbing",2026-04-22,Gecontacteerd
Peter van Dijk,peter@example.nl,+31698765432,Elektrische diensten,"Damstraat 789, Amsterdam","Office wiring",2026-04-25,Geoffreerd

Visual Excel Layout (Columns A-H):
┌─────────┬─────────┬─────────┬─────────┬─────────┬─────────┬─────────┬─────────┐
│    A    │    B    │    C    │    D    │    E    │    F    │    G    │    H    │
├─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┤
│fullName │  email  │  phone  │ service │ address │ project │  date   │ status  │
│         │         │         │  Type   │         │ Details │Received │         │
├─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┼─────────┤
│Jan de   │jan@...  │+3161... │Dakdek.. │Kouw...  │Need...  │04/20/26 │In       │
│Vries    │         │         │kersdien │         │         │         │afwacht..│
└─────────┴─────────┴─────────┴─────────┴─────────┴─────────┴─────────┴─────────┘

Without optional fields (will use current date and PENDING status):
fullName,email,phone,serviceType,address,projectDetails,dateReceived,status
John Smith,john@example.nl,+31612345678,Dakdekkersdiensten,"Dam 1, Amsterdam","Urgent repair",,

========================================
📥 UPLOAD REQUIREMENTS
========================================

• File format: CSV (.csv) or Excel (.xlsx)
• Maximum file size: 25MB
• Encoding: UTF-8 recommended (for CSV)
• First row MUST be headers
• Use quotes for fields with commas (CSV only)
• Download template: lead-upload-template.csv

💡 XLSX ADVANTAGES:
• Dates auto-convert (Excel date format → YYYY-MM-DD)
• Phone numbers: +31612345678 or 31612345678 (+ added automatically)
• No encoding issues with Dutch characters
• No comma/quote escaping needed

========================================
✅ SUCCESS RESPONSE (200)
========================================

{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Upload completed. 3 leads created, 0 failed.",
  "data": {
    "totalProcessed": 3,
    "successCount": 3,
    "failureCount": 0,
    "successful": [
      {
        "row": 2,
        "leadId": "uuid-1",
        "fullName": "Jan de Vries",
        "email": "jan@example.nl"
      },
      {
        "row": 3,
        "leadId": "uuid-2",
        "fullName": "Maria Jansen",
        "email": "maria@example.nl"
      }
    ],
    "failed": []
  }
}

========================================
❌ ERROR HANDLING
========================================

If some rows fail, response includes both:
• successful: Array of created leads
• failed: Array of errors with row numbers

Example with errors:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Upload completed. 2 leads created, 1 failed.",
  "data": {
    "totalProcessed": 3,
    "successCount": 2,
    "failureCount": 1,
    "successful": [...],
    "failed": [
      {
        "row": 4,
        "data": {...},
        "error": "Invalid service type: 'InvalidType'. Must be one of: ..."
      }
    ]
  }
}

Common errors:
• Missing required fields
• Invalid service type (not in Dutch list)
• Invalid email format
• Invalid date format (must be YYYY-MM-DD, e.g., 2026-04-20)
• Invalid status (must be English: PENDING/CONTACTED/QUOTED/COMPLETED/CLOSED or Dutch: In afwachting/Gecontacteerd/Geoffreerd/Voltooid/Gesloten)
• Duplicate email (if exists)

========================================
💡 FRONTEND USAGE TIPS
========================================

1. Provide template CSV download
2. Show service type dropdown/list
3. Validate CSV before upload (optional)
4. Show progress during upload
5. Display success/failure summary
6. Allow retry for failed rows
7. Highlight row numbers with errors

========================================
📊 WORKFLOW
========================================

1. Admin downloads template CSV
2. Admin fills Excel/CSV with lead data (with optional status column)
3. Admin uploads CSV or XLSX via this endpoint
4. Backend validates each row
5. Creates leads with provided status (or PENDING if not specified)
6. Returns success/failure report
7. Admin fixes failed rows and re-uploads

========================================
⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES
========================================

• Default status: PENDING (if not provided in CSV/XLSX)
• Custom status: Can specify in English (PENDING, CONTACTED, QUOTED, COMPLETED, CLOSED) or Dutch (In afwachting, Gecontacteerd, Geoffreerd, Voltooid, Gesloten)
• Status values in CSV/XLSX: Both English and Dutch accepted
• Reference images NOT supported in CSV/XLSX
• Use individual lead creation for images
• Row numbers in errors start from 2 (row 1 is header)
• Process continues even if some rows fail
• Successful leads are created immediately

Export all leads to Excel file (Admin only).

========================================
📥 EXPORT LEADS TO EXCEL
========================================

Downloads all leads from the database as an Excel (.xlsx) file.

✅ FEATURES:
• Exports all leads in one Excel file
• Includes all lead information (name, email, phone, service, address, etc.)
• Formatted columns with proper widths
• Automatic filename: leads-export-YYYY-MM-DD.xlsx
• Ready to open in Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, LibreOffice

========================================
📊 EXPORTED COLUMNS
========================================

The Excel file contains these columns (in order):

1. # - Row number (for easy reference)
2. Full Name - Customer full name
3. Email - Customer email address
4. Phone - Customer phone number
5. Service Type - Service requested (English)
6. Address - Project address
7. Project Details - Project description
8. Date Received - Date when lead was received (YYYY-MM-DD)
9. Status - Current lead status (PENDING, CONTACTED, QUOTED, COMPLETED, CLOSED)
10. Created At - Lead creation timestamp

========================================
✅ SUCCESS RESPONSE (200)
========================================

Returns Excel file as download:
• Content-Type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet
• Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="leads-export-2026-04-28.xlsx"
• File downloads automatically in browser/Postman

In Postman:
1. Click "Send"
2. File downloads to your Downloads folder
3. Open with Excel/Sheets

========================================
❌ ERROR RESPONSES
========================================

404 - No leads found:
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "No leads found to export"
}

500 - Export failed:
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Failed to export leads"
}

========================================
💡 USE CASES
========================================

1. **Backup:** Regular backups of all lead data
2. **Reporting:** Share leads with team/management
3. **Analysis:** Analyze leads in Excel (pivot tables, charts)
4. **Migration:** Move data to another system
5. **Offline Access:** Work with leads offline

========================================
📝 EXCEL FILE EXAMPLE
========================================

| # | Full Name    | Email           | Phone        | Service Type | Status    |
|---|--------------|-----------------|--------------|--------------|----------|
| 1 | John Smith   | john@example.nl | +31612345678 | PLUMBING     | PENDING  |
| 2 | Sarah Johnson| sarah@example.nl| +31687654321 | ROOFING      | CONTACTED|
| 3 | Mike Ross    | mike@example.nl | +31698765432 | ELECTRICAL   | QUOTED   |

========================================
🔄 WORKFLOW
========================================

1. Admin clicks "Export Leads" button
   ↓
2. GET /api/admin/leads/export
   ↓
3. Backend fetches all leads from database
   ↓
4. Converts to Excel format (.xlsx)
   ↓
5. Returns file for download
   ↓
6. Admin opens file in Excel
   ↓
7. Admin can analyze, share, or backup data

========================================
⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES
========================================

• Exports ALL leads (no pagination)
• Large databases may take a few seconds
• File size depends on number of leads
• Reference images NOT included (URLs only)
• Service types shown in English (database format)
• Timestamps in local time format

Delete a lead permanently.

URL PARAMETERS:
:id - Lead UUID

RESPONSE:
✅ 200 - Lead deleted successfully
❌ 404 - Lead not found

WARNING: This action is irreversible. Consider setting status to CLOSED instead.

item.description: Lead management endpoints - view, update status, and delete contact form submissions

item.name: dashboard

request.description:
Get dashboard overview data including total leads, active giveaways, revenue, and recent quote requests.

========================================
📊 RETURNS
========================================

• **totalLeads:** Total number of leads in the system
• **leadsThisMonth:** Count of leads created this month
• **activeGiveaways:** Count of giveaways with ACTIVE status
• **totalTicketsSold:** Total tickets sold across all giveaways
• **revenueThisMonth:** Total revenue from completed orders this month (in euros)
• **recentLeads:** Array of 5 most recent leads with Dutch status names
  - id, fullName, serviceType, status, createdAt

========================================
✅ EXAMPLE RESPONSE
========================================

{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "totalLeads": 1284,
    "leadsThisMonth": 152,
    "activeGiveaways": 1,
    "totalTicketsSold": 842,
    "revenueThisMonth": "45200.00",
    "recentLeads": [
      {
        "id": "uuid",
        "fullName": "Jane Smith",
        "serviceType": "Kitchen Remodel",
        "status": "In afwachting",
        "createdAt": "2026-04-28T10:30:00Z"
      }
    ]
  }
}

Get only active giveaways with ticket packages and progress information.

========================================
📊 RETURNS
========================================

Array of active giveaways containing:
• id, title, description
• totalTickets, ticketsSold
• drawDate, bannerImage
• packages (array): id, title, couponCount, price

========================================
✅ EXAMPLE RESPONSE
========================================

{
  "success": true,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "title": "Bathroom Renovation Giveaway",
      "description": "Win a complete luxury master bathroom remodel worth €15,000",
      "totalTickets": 1000,
      "ticketsSold": 842,
      "drawDate": "2026-05-10T00:00:00Z",
      "bannerImage": "/uploads/giveaway-123.jpg",
      "packages": [
        {
          "id": "pkg-uuid",
          "title": "1 Ticket",
          "couponCount": 1,
          "price": "50.00"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

Export all quote requests/leads to Excel format with Dutch column headers.

========================================
📋 EXPORT FORMAT
========================================

Excel file (.xlsx) with columns:
• # (row number)
• Volledige Naam (Full Name)
• E-mailadres (Email)
• Telefoonnummer (Phone)
• Diensttype (Service Type)
• Adres (Address)
• Projectdetails (Project Details)
• Datum Ontvangen (Date Received) - in DD-MM-YYYY format
• Status (in Dutch - In afwachting, Gecontacteerd, etc.)
• Aangemaakt op (Created Date/Time) - in DD-MM-YYYY HH:MM:SS format

========================================
✅ RESPONSE
========================================

• Downloads as: offerteverzoeken_YYYY-MM-DD.xlsx
• Content-Type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet
• File contains all leads sorted by creation date (newest first)

========================================
💡 USAGE
========================================

• Click Send to download the Excel file automatically
• Use for reporting and data analysis
• All labels and statuses are in Dutch

item.description: Admin dashboard endpoints - overview statistics, active giveaways, and lead export

item.name: coupons

request.description:
Get coupon overview/statistics for admin dashboard.

========================================
📊 RETURNS
========================================

• **totalCoupons:** Total number of coupons generated
• **validCoupons:** Count of coupons with VALID status (not used/cancelled)
• **voidCoupons:** Count of coupons with VOID status (cancelled/used)
• **usageRate:** Percentage of coupons that have been used (0-100%)
• **totalParticipants:** Count of unique users who purchased coupons
• **totalRevenue:** Sum of all completed order amounts (€)
• **averageRevenuePerOrder:** Average revenue per completed order (€)
• **completedOrders:** Count of COMPLETED (paid) orders
• **recentCoupons:** Last 5 generated coupons with details (code, status, user, giveaway)

========================================
✅ EXAMPLE RESPONSE
========================================

{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Coupon overview retrieved successfully",
  "data": {
    "totalCoupons": 850,
    "validCoupons": 750,
    "voidCoupons": 100,
    "usageRate": 88.24,
    "totalParticipants": 642,
    "totalRevenue": 42500.00,
    "averageRevenuePerOrder": 315.43,
    "completedOrders": 135,
    "recentCoupons": [
      {
        "id": "coupon-uuid-1",
        "couponCode": "GW-A1B2C3-001",
        "status": "VALID",
        "createdAt": "2026-05-03T10:15:00Z",
        "user": {
          "fullName": "Marcus Wright",
          "email": "m.wright@structura.io"
        },
        "giveaway": {
          "title": "Bathroom Renovation Giveaway"
        }
      },
      {
        "id": "coupon-uuid-2",
        "couponCode": "GW-D4E5F6-002",
        "status": "VOID",
        "createdAt": "2026-05-03T10:10:00Z",
        "user": {
          "fullName": "Sarah Lopez",
          "email": "sarah.lopez@example.com"
        },
        "giveaway": {
          "title": "Bathroom Renovation Giveaway"
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}

========================================
💡 USE CASE
========================================
Use this endpoint to populate dashboard cards:
- "Total Tickets Sold" card: Use totalCoupons
- "Total Participants" card: Use totalParticipants
- "Revenue Generated" card: Use totalRevenue
- "Coupon Usage Rate" card: Use usageRate
- Display recent coupons table

query.description: Filter by start date (YYYY-MM-DD format, optional)
Filter by end date (YYYY-MM-DD format, optional)
Filter by order status: COMPLETED or REFUNDED (optional, default: both)

request.description:
Get all coupons grouped by participant email (Admin only).

This endpoint returns all participants who have purchased coupons, with their order details and coupon information.

⚠️ SUPPORTS FILTERING: Date range and status filters available via query parameters.

========================================
🔍 QUERY PARAMETERS (All Optional)
========================================

• **startDate** (optional)
  - Format: YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 2023-10-01)
  - Filters orders from this date onwards (inclusive)
  - Example: ?startDate=2023-10-01

• **endDate** (optional)
  - Format: YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 2023-10-31)
  - Filters orders up to this date (inclusive, includes full day)
  - Example: ?endDate=2023-10-31

• **status** (optional)
  - Values: COMPLETED or REFUNDED
  - Filters by order payment status
  - Default: Both COMPLETED and REFUNDED
  - Example: ?status=COMPLETED

📋 **Filter Examples:**

1. All orders in October 2023:
   GET /api/admin/coupons?startDate=2023-10-01&endDate=2023-10-31

2. Only completed (paid) orders:
   GET /api/admin/coupons?status=COMPLETED

3. Completed orders in date range:
   GET /api/admin/coupons?startDate=2023-10-01&endDate=2023-10-31&status=COMPLETED

4. Orders from specific date onwards:
   GET /api/admin/coupons?startDate=2023-10-12

5. Orders up to specific date:
   GET /api/admin/coupons?endDate=2023-10-15

========================================
📊 RESPONSE STRUCTURE
========================================

Returns participants grouped by email, each showing:
• Email address (unique identifier)
• Full name
• Phone number
• Instagram username
• Initials (first letters of name)
• Total orders count
• Total coupons purchased
• Total amount spent
• Array of all orders with coupon details

========================================
📋 PARTICIPANT DATA
========================================

Each participant object contains:

1. **Basic Info:**
   - email: Participant email address
   - fullName: Participant full name
   - phone: Phone number
   - instagram: Instagram username
   - initials: First letters of name (e.g., "MW" for Marcus Wright)

2. **Summary:**
   - totalOrders: Number of orders placed
   - totalCoupons: Total coupons purchased across all orders
   - totalSpent: Total amount spent (€)

3. **Orders Array:**
   Each order contains:
   - orderId: Order UUID
   - giveawayName: Name of the giveaway
   - purchaseDate: When the order was placed
   - quantity: Number of coupons in this order
   - totalPrice: Order amount (€)
   - status: COMPLETED or REFUNDED
   - couponCodes: Array of coupon codes (e.g., ["GW-A1B2C3-001", "GW-A1B2C3-002"])

========================================
✅ SUCCESS RESPONSE (200)
========================================

{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Coupons retrieved successfully",
  "data": {
    "totalParticipants": 4,
    "participants": [
      {
        "email": "m.wright@structura.io",
        "fullName": "Marcus Wright",
        "phone": "(+07) 555-0135",
        "instagram": "@wright_builds",
        "initials": "MW",
        "totalOrders": 2,
        "totalCoupons": 5,
        "totalSpent": 250.00,
        "orders": [
          {
            "orderId": "uuid-1",
            "giveawayName": "€10,000 Cash Giveaway",
            "purchaseDate": "2023-10-12T14:20:00Z",
            "quantity": 3,
            "totalPrice": 150.00,
            "status": "COMPLETED",
            "couponCodes": [
              "RTK-4521",
              "RTK-4522",
              "RTK-4523"
            ]
          },
          {
            "orderId": "uuid-2",
            "giveawayName": "€10,000 Cash Giveaway",
            "purchaseDate": "2023-10-13T09:12:00Z",
            "quantity": 2,
            "totalPrice": 100.00,
            "status": "REFUNDED",
            "couponCodes": [
              "RTK-4524",
              "RTK-4933"
            ]
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "email": "sarah.lopez@example.com",
        "fullName": "Sarah Lopez",
        "phone": "+31612345678",
        "instagram": "@lopez_design",
        "initials": "SL",
        "totalOrders": 1,
        "totalCoupons": 2,
        "totalSpent": 100.00,
        "orders": [
          {
            "orderId": "uuid-3",
            "giveawayName": "€10,000 Cash Giveaway",
            "purchaseDate": "2023-10-13T15:05:00Z",
            "quantity": 2,
            "totalPrice": 100.00,
            "status": "COMPLETED",
            "couponCodes": [
              "RTK-5001",
              "RTK-5002"
            ]
          }
        ]
      }
    ],
    "filters": {
      "startDate": "2023-10-01",
      "endDate": "2023-10-31",
      "status": "COMPLETED"
    }
  }
}

========================================
💡 USE CASES
========================================

1. **Admin Dashboard:**
   - Display all participants in a table
   - Show participant count, total sales
   - Filter by date range (Date Range picker)
   - Filter by status (All Status dropdown)

2. **Participant Management:**
   - View all buyers in specific period
   - Track purchase history
   - Identify refunded orders

3. **Statistics & Reporting:**
   - Monthly sales reports
   - Date-specific analysis
   - Revenue tracking by period

========================================
🎯 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION
========================================

Filter UI:
```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Filters:  [All Status ▼]  [📅 Date Range]           │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Display as a table:

| GIVEAWAY NAME          | PARTICIPANT | INSTAGRAM       | PURCHASE DATE | QTY | TOTAL PRICE | STATUS    | ACTIONS      |
|------------------------|-------------|-----------------|---------------|-----|-------------|-----------|-------------|
| €10,000 Cash Giveaway  | MW          | @wright_builds  | Oct 12, 2023  | 5   | €250.00     | Paid      | View Details|
| €10,000 Cash Giveaway  | SL          | @lopez_design   | Oct 13, 2023  | 2   | €100.00     | Paid      | View Details|
| €10,000 Cash Giveaway  | AB          | @anna_builds_it | Oct 13, 2023  | 1   | €50.00      | Paid      | View Details|

"View Details" opens modal with individual participant's coupons (see Get Coupons by Email endpoint)

Frontend Code Example:
```javascript
const fetchCoupons = async (filters) => {
  const params = new URLSearchParams();
  if (filters.startDate) params.append('startDate', filters.startDate);
  if (filters.endDate) params.append('endDate', filters.endDate);
  if (filters.status) params.append('status', filters.status);
  
  const response = await fetch(`/api/admin/coupons?${params}`, {
    headers: { 'Authorization': `Bearer ${token}` }
  });
  return await response.json();
};

// Example usage:
fetchCoupons({ startDate: '2023-10-01', endDate: '2023-10-31', status: 'COMPLETED' });
```

========================================
⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES
========================================

• Default: Returns both COMPLETED and REFUNDED orders
• Date filtering: Based on order creation date (purchaseDate)
• Date format: YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 2023-10-12)
• End date includes full day (until 23:59:59)
• Participants are sorted by most recent purchase first
• Multiple orders from same email are grouped together
• Each order shows individual coupon codes
• Status is at ORDER level (not coupon level)
• Coupon codes are unique (e.g., GW-A1B2C3-001)
• Applied filters returned in response for frontend sync

Get all coupons for a specific participant by email (Admin only).

This endpoint returns detailed information about a participant and all their coupons across all orders.

========================================
📊 RESPONSE STRUCTURE
========================================

Returns complete participant profile with:
• Personal information (email, name, phone, instagram)
• Purchase summary (total spent)
• All orders with individual coupon details
• Each coupon shows: ID, coupon code, status, creation date

========================================
📋 PARTICIPANT DETAILS
========================================

Response includes:

1. **Basic Info:**
   - email: Participant email
   - fullName: Full name
   - phone: Phone number
   - instagram: Instagram username
   - initials: Name initials (e.g., "MW")
   - totalSpent: Total amount spent across all orders (€)

2. **Orders with Coupons:**
   Each order contains:
   - orderId: Order UUID
   - giveawayName: Giveaway title
   - purchaseDate: Order date/time
   - quantity: Number of coupons
   - totalPrice: Order amount (€)
   - status: COMPLETED or REFUNDED
   - coupons: Array of individual coupon objects

3. **Individual Coupons:**
   Each coupon shows:
   - id: Coupon UUID
   - couponCode: Unique code (e.g., "RTK-4521")
   - status: VALID or VOID
   - createdAt: When coupon was generated

========================================
🔗 URL PARAMETERS
========================================

:email - Participant email address (required)
  Example: m.wright@structura.io

========================================
✅ SUCCESS RESPONSE (200)
========================================

{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Participant coupons retrieved successfully",
  "data": {
    "email": "m.wright@structura.io",
    "fullName": "Marcus Wright",
    "phone": "(+07) 555-0135",
    "instagram": "@wright_builds",
    "initials": "MW",
    "totalSpent": 250.00,
    "orders": [
      {
        "orderId": "uuid-1",
        "giveawayName": "€10,000 Cash Giveaway",
        "purchaseDate": "2023-10-12T14:20:00Z",
        "quantity": 3,
        "totalPrice": 150.00,
        "status": "COMPLETED",
        "coupons": [
          {
            "id": "coupon-uuid-1",
            "couponCode": "RTK-4521",
            "status": "VALID",
            "createdAt": "2023-10-12T14:21:00Z"
          },
          {
            "id": "coupon-uuid-2",
            "couponCode": "RTK-4522",
            "status": "VALID",
            "createdAt": "2023-10-12T14:21:01Z"
          },
          {
            "id": "coupon-uuid-3",
            "couponCode": "RTK-4523",
            "status": "VALID",
            "createdAt": "2023-10-12T14:21:02Z"
          }
        ]
      },
      {
        "orderId": "uuid-2",
        "giveawayName": "€10,000 Cash Giveaway",
        "purchaseDate": "2023-10-13T09:12:00Z",
        "quantity": 2,
        "totalPrice": 100.00,
        "status": "REFUNDED",
        "coupons": [
          {
            "id": "coupon-uuid-4",
            "couponCode": "RTK-4524",
            "status": "VALID",
            "createdAt": "2023-10-13T09:13:00Z"
          },
          {
            "id": "coupon-uuid-5",
            "couponCode": "RTK-4933",
            "status": "VALID",
            "createdAt": "2023-10-13T09:13:01Z"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}

========================================
❌ ERROR RESPONSES
========================================

400 - Missing email parameter:
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Email parameter is required"
}

404 - Participant not found:
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Participant not found"
}

500 - Server error:
{
  "success": false,
  "error": "Failed to retrieve participant coupons"
}

========================================
💡 USE CASES
========================================

1. **View Details Modal:**
   - Triggered from "View Details" button in main table
   - Shows participant info at top
   - Lists all "Active Coupons" with codes
   - Shows payment status for each order

2. **Participant Profile:**
   - Complete purchase history
   - All coupon codes owned
   - Refund tracking

3. **Customer Support:**
   - Verify participant's coupons
   - Check coupon status
   - Handle refund requests

========================================
🎯 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION
========================================

Modal/Popup Display:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  👤 Marcus Wright              [X]      │
│  📧 m.wright@structura.io               │
│  📱 (+07) 555-0135                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Active Coupons:                        │
│                                         │
│  🎫 RTK-4521    Expires 2023            │
│  🎫 RTK-4522    Expires 2023            │
│  🎫 RTK-4523    Expires 2023            │
│  🎫 RTK-4524    Expires 2023            │
│  🎫 RTK-4933    Expires 2023            │
│                                         │
│  Payment Status: ✅ Paid                │
│  Purchase Date: 12 Oct. 2023 14:20      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘

========================================
⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES
========================================

• Email parameter is case-sensitive
• Returns all orders (COMPLETED and REFUNDED)
• Each order lists individual coupons
• Coupon status: VALID (active) or VOID (cancelled)
• All coupons shown regardless of order status
• Most recent orders appear first

item.description: Coupon management endpoints - view all participants and their coupons
Admin-only endpoints for managing authentication, giveaways, vouchers, services, leads, and coupons

item.name: Public
Vouchers

request.description:
Validate a voucher code (PUBLIC - No authentication required).

REQUEST BODY (Raw JSON):
{
  "code": "AMBER-FALL-27",
  "orderAmount": 500
}

FIELDS:
• code (string) - Voucher code to validate
• orderAmount (number) - Total order amount before discount

CHECKS:
1. Voucher exists and is ACTIVE
2. Not expired (expirationDate > now)
3. Has remaining uses (usedCount < usageLimit)

RETURNS:
• voucherCode - Same code (send this when creating order, NOT an ID)
• discountAmount - Amount of discount
• finalAmount - Order amount after discount
• remainingUses - How many uses left

IMPORTANT:
✅ This endpoint only PREVIEWS the discount
❌ Does NOT consume the voucher (usedCount stays the same)

VOUCHER CONSUMPTION:
Happens automatically server-side when:
1. Customer completes payment
2. Frontend sends voucherCode (not ID) to order endpoint
3. Backend validates, creates order, and consumes voucher automatically

No need to call this API again after payment!

item.description: Public voucher endpoints - no authentication required

item.name: active-giveaway

request.description:
Get the current active giveaway with full details and ticket packages.

RETURNS:
• Complete giveaway object with all packages
• Packages are ordered by couponCount (ascending)
• Full details: id, title, description, totalTickets, ticketsSold, drawDate, bannerImage, status, createdAt, updatedAt
• All ticket packages with: id, couponCount, price, title, badgeText, savePercentage

USE CASE:
• Homepage giveaway display
• Main giveaway page
• Ticket purchase flow

RESPONSE:
✅ 200 - Active giveaway with packages
❌ 404 - No active giveaway found

NOTE: Only one giveaway can be ACTIVE at a time. Returns full data without pagination.

item.description: Public giveaway endpoint - no authentication required, returns current ACTIVE giveaway

item.name: orders

request.description:
Create order and get Stripe checkout URL.

REQUEST BODY (Raw JSON):
{
  "packageId": "uuid-from-giveaway",
  "voucherCode": "AMBER-FALL-24",  // OPTIONAL
  "fullName": "John Doe",
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "phone": "+31612345678",
  "instagramUsername": "johndoe"  // OPTIONAL
}

REQUIRED FIELDS:
• packageId - Get from active giveaway packages
• fullName - Customer full name
• email - Customer email
• phone - Customer phone number

OPTIONAL FIELDS:
• voucherCode - Promo code for discount
• instagramUsername - Customer Instagram handle

RETURNS:
• orderId - Order reference ID
• sessionId - Stripe session ID
• paymentUrl - Redirect user to this URL for payment ← IMPORTANT!
• totalAmount - Final amount after discount
• originalAmount - Price before discount
• discountAmount - Amount saved

NEXT STEPS:
1. Redirect user to paymentUrl
2. User completes payment on Stripe hosted page
3. Stripe redirects back to success_url with session_id
4. Call Confirm Order endpoint with session_id

RESPONSE:
✅ 201 - Order created, redirect to paymentUrl
❌ 400 - Invalid package/voucher or not enough tickets

NOTE: Order status is PENDING until payment confirmed

Confirm payment and complete order. Generates individual coupons.

REQUEST BODY (Raw JSON):
{
  "sessionId": "cs_test_xxxxxxxxxxxxx"
}

FIELDS:
• sessionId - From Stripe success_url query parameter

WHEN TO CALL:
After user completes payment on Stripe, they are redirected to:
`http://localhost:3001/order/success?session_id=cs_test_xxx&order_id=uuid`

Extract session_id from URL and call this endpoint.

WHAT HAPPENS:
1. Verifies payment with Stripe API
2. Updates order status to COMPLETED
3. Generates individual coupons (e.g., 20 coupons for 20-ticket package)
4. Increments voucher usage (if voucher used)
5. Increments giveaway ticketsSold
6. Updates user totalSpent

COUPON GENERATION:
• If package has 1 coupon → generates 1 Coupon record
• If package has 20 coupons → generates 20 Coupon records
• Each coupon gets unique code: GW-XXXXXX-001, GW-XXXXXX-002, etc.
• More coupons = more entries in draw = better chance of winning!

RETURNS:
• Complete order details
• All generated coupons with unique codes
• Updated giveaway stats

RESPONSE:
✅ 200 - Payment confirmed, coupons generated
❌ 400 - Session not found, already completed, or payment not successful

NOTE: Each coupon is a separate entry in the giveaway draw

Get order details with all coupons.

URL PARAMETERS:
:orderId - Order UUID

RETURNS:
• Complete order information
• Package details
• Giveaway details
• Voucher details (if used)
• User information
• All generated coupons (if order completed)

USE CASE:
• Check order status
• View purchased coupons
• Order confirmation page
• Customer support

RESPONSE:
✅ 200 - Order details retrieved
❌ 404 - Order not found

item.description: Public order endpoints - create orders, process Stripe payments, generate coupons

item.name: leads

formdata.description: Type dienst in Dutch - see valid values in docs (required)

formdata.value: Beschrijf uw project, tijdlijn en eventuele specifieke voorsten...

request.description:
Submit contact form for quote request (lead collection). PUBLIC - No authentication required.

FORM-DATA FIELDS:
• fullName (text) - Customer full name (required)
• email (text) - Customer email address (required)
• phone (text) - Customer phone number (required)
• serviceType (text) - Service type in Dutch (required). Valid values:
  - Loodgietersdiensten
  - Dakdekkersdiensten
  - Stukadoorsdienst
  - Elektrische diensten
  - Tegelservices
  - Complete vloeroplossingen op één plek
  - Schoonmaakdiensten
  - Algemene service
• address (text) - Project address (required)
• projectDetails (text) - Project description and details (optional)
• referenceImages (files) - Reference images for the project (optional, max 5 files, 5MB each)

PROCESS:
1. Frontend sends Dutch service type (e.g., 'Dakdekkersdiensten')
2. Backend converts to English enum (e.g., 'ROOFING_SERVICES') for storage
3. Backend returns Dutch name in response

RESPONSE:
✅ 201 - Lead submitted successfully
• Success message in Dutch: "Uw offerteverzoek is succesvol ingediend. We nemen binnenkort contact met u op!"
• Lead data with Dutch serviceType
• Reference image URLs

❌ 400 - Validation error (missing required fields or invalid service type)
• Error messages in Dutch

NOTE: This endpoint is public and does not require authentication. Lead status is automatically set to PENDING.

item.description: Public lead/contact form endpoint - collect customer quote requests

item.name: Public - Services

request.description:
Get all active services for display on the frontend. PUBLIC - No authentication required.

========================================
📋 RESPONSE STRUCTURE
========================================

Returns all ACTIVE services with:
• Service details (name, description, basePrice)
• Category name in Dutch
• Absolute image URLs (ready to display)
• Service slug for routing

========================================
✅ SUCCESS RESPONSE (200)
========================================

{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Services retrieved successfully",
  "data": {
    "totalServices": 8,
    "services": [
      {
        "id": "uuid-1",
        "name": "Complete Loodgieterservices",
        "slug": "complete-loodgieterservices",
        "description": "Van leidinglekken tot volledige badkamerinstallaties, wij bieden complete loodgieteroplossingen voor zowel woningen als commerciële panden. Wij houden ons ook bezig met het lekdichte maken van leidingen, rioolinstallatie en verzorgen afwerking.",
        "basePrice": 150.00,
        "category": "Loodgietersdiensten",
        "bannerImage": "http://localhost:3000/uploads/plumbing-banner.jpg",
        "galleryImages": [
          "http://localhost:3000/uploads/plumbing-1.jpg",
          "http://localhost:3000/uploads/plumbing-2.jpg",
          "http://localhost:3000/uploads/plumbing-3.jpg"
        ],
        "createdAt": "2026-04-15T10:00:00Z"
      },
      {
        "id": "uuid-2",
        "name": "Professionele Dakdekkersdiensten",
        "slug": "professionele-dakdekkersdiensten",
        "description": "Wij zijn gespecialiseerd in complete dakoplossingen, inclusief zowel platte als hellende daken, dakreparaties, dakbedekking en zinken afwerking. Van lekdetectie tot volledige dakvernieuwing.",
        "basePrice": 200.00,
        "category": "Dakdekkersdiensten",
        "bannerImage": "http://localhost:3000/uploads/roofing-banner.jpg",
        "galleryImages": [
          "http://localhost:3000/uploads/roofing-1.jpg",
          "http://localhost:3000/uploads/roofing-2.jpg"
        ],
        "createdAt": "2026-04-15T10:05:00Z"
      }
    ]
  }
}

========================================
💡 USE CASES
========================================

1. **Services Page:**
   - Display all services in a grid
   - Show service cards with banner images
   - Link to individual service detail pages

2. **Contact Form:**
   - Populate service type dropdown
   - Use Dutch category names for display

3. **Homepage:**
   - Feature top services
   - Show service categories

========================================
🎯 FRONTEND IMPLEMENTATION
========================================

Display as service cards (as shown in your reference image):

```javascript
const fetchServices = async () => {
  const response = await fetch('/api/services');
  const { data } = await response.json();
  
  return data.services; // Array of service objects
};

// Render service card
services.forEach(service => {
  console.log(service.name); // "Complete Loodgieterservices"
  console.log(service.category); // "Loodgietersdiensten" (Dutch)
  console.log(service.bannerImage); // Full absolute URL
  console.log(service.basePrice); // 150.00
});
```

========================================
⚠️ IMPORTANT NOTES
========================================

• Only returns ACTIVE services (not DRAFT or COMPLETED)
• Category names are in Dutch (frontend-ready)
• All image URLs are absolute (ready to display)
• Services sorted by creation date (newest first)
• No pagination (all services returned)
• Public endpoint (no authentication required)

item.description: Public service endpoints - display services on frontend
Public endpoints accessible to all users without authentication
